slides.title: What is a Modchip?
slides.body:
A modchip is a small circuit board soldered onto your Switch's motherboard.

This allows patched consoles, Switch Lite, and Switch OLED - hardware that cannot use RCM - to run custom firmware like Atmosphere.

The best modchip is the Picofly.

slides.title: Supported Hardware
slides.body:
Modchips are available for every Switch model:

  PicoFly:
    - Original Switch (patched & unpatched)
    - Switch Lite
    - Switch OLED

Unpatched consoles don't need a modchip, but using one delivers a better experience

slides.title: Installation
slides.body:
Installation requires soldering to tiny points on the motherboard.

Difficulty varies by model:
  - Original Switch: Low
  - Switch Lite: Medium
  - Switch OLED: High

The PicoFly both include installation guides and training mode LEDs that confirm correct installation.
slides.note: A single bad solder joint can permanently damage your console. If you're not confident, find a local repair shop experienced with microsoldering.

slides.title: After Installation
slides.body:
Once installed, a modchipped Switch will boot Hekate automatically on every power-on.
  - Atmosphere and most homebrew work exactly the same.

The usk modchip firmware in the Essentials tab keeps your PicoFly firmware up to date directly from the Switch SD card.
